headline: Kraft Heinz Rebuilds Core Brands As Berkshire Backs Turnaround Valuation Story
summary: Kraft Heinz has dropped its breakup plan and will keep the company intact. New CEO Steve Cahillane is committing about $600 million to refresh core brands and products. Berkshire Hathaway has backed the new approach, with leaders including Greg Abel reversing an earlier plan to sell shares. The company is using new products and brand updates to try to win back market share in a competitive food sector. Kraft Heinz, NasdaqGS:KHC, is making this shift while its shares trade around $22.79.
